bead.title: Frame future service-backed multi-node ddx control-plane topology
bead.description:
<context-digest>
Review area: future ddx server control-plane topology. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, and the requirement for a central UI/storage layer with multiple headless servers managing many projects and workers.
</context-digest>
Frame the post-local topology for DDx after the one-machine multi-project server lands.

## Goals
1. Define the boundary between git-authoritative project state and any future service-backed storage/read model
2. Describe a topology with one central storage/control service, one central web UI, and multiple headless `ddx server` node gateways
3. Define what a node gateway owns: project discovery/registration, worker supervision, local execution, and synchronization with the central service
4. Define failure modes, degraded operation, and migration path so local multi-project servers continue to work when the central service is unavailable
5. Keep this as framing only until the one-machine multi-project module is designed and accepted
bead.acceptance: A framing design exists for the future service-backed DDx topology, defining central storage/UI vs headless node-gateway responsibilities, git-authoritative vs service-backed data boundaries, degraded local operation when the service is unavailable, and migration from the one-machine multi-project server stage
